title: Chamois with integrated pouch
abstract: A chamois for a cycling garment includes a padded rear portion for cushioning an area of a user of the garment, and a front portion. The front portion includes a front panel and a resiliently elastic stretch panel attached to the front panel. The stretch panel defines, with the front panel, a pouch configured to receive therein the user's genitalia. The stretch panel includes an opening providing access to the pouch.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A chamois for a cycling garment, comprising: a padded rear portion for cushioning an area of a user of the garment; and a front portion comprising: a front panel; and a resiliently elastic stretch panel attached to the front panel, defining with the front panel a pouch configured to receive therein the userâs genitalia, and comprising an opening providing access to the pouch. 2. The chamois of claim 1 , wherein the area is one or both of a crotch and a buttocks of the user. 3. The chamois of claim 1 or 2, wherein the front panel comprises foam. 4. The chamois of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the front panel is perforated for increasing a breathability of the front panel. 5. The chamois of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the front portion further comprises lateral wing portions attached to edges of the stretch panel and the front panel. 6. The chamois of claim 5, wherein the lateral wing portions comprise foam. 7. The chamois of claim 5 or 6, wherein the lateral wing portions are monolithically formed with the rear portion. 8. The chamois of any one of claims 1-7, wherein: the front panel and the stretch panel are attached to one another at a seam; and the padded rear portion comprises padding that does not extend beyond the seam. 9. A garment for use when cycling, comprising: a body including leg openings for a userâs legs; and a chamois attached to the body and comprising: a padded chamois rear portion for cushioning an area of the user; a front chamois portion comprising: a front panel; and 

 a resiliently elastic stretch panel attached to the front panel, defining with the front panel a pouch configured to receive therein the userâs genitalia, and comprising an opening providing access to the pouch. 10. The garment of claim 9, wherein the garment does not comprise a waistband. 11. The garment of claim 9 or 10, wherein the body comprises: a garment front portion attached to the chamois front portion; and a garment rear portion attached to the chamois rear portion, wherein at least a portion of an edge of the chamois is disconnected from the body. 12. The garment of claim 11 , wherein: the chamois front portion further comprises lateral wing portions attached to edges of the stretch panel and the front panel; and the edge of the chamois that is disconnected from the body comprises at least a portion of edges of the lateral wing portions. 13. The garment of claim 12, wherein the garment front portion is further attached to at least a portion of the lateral wing portions. 14. The garment of any one of claims 9-13, wherein at least a portion of edges of the stretch panel and the front panel are not attached to the body such that the pouch is free-hanging. 15. The garment of claim 14, wherein the garment front portion is connected to the front panel via only at least a portion of a front edge of the front panel. 16. The garment of any one of claims 9-15, wherein an open space is provided between the chamois and the body, the open space extending from beyond a left side of the chamois to beyond a right side of the chamois. 17. A method of making a garment for use when cycling, comprising: providing a body including leg openings for a userâs legs; and attaching a chamois the body, the chamois comprising: a padded rear portion for cushioning an area of the user; and a front portion comprising: a front panel; and 

 a resiliently elastic stretch panel connected to the front panel, defining with the front panel a pouch configured to receive therein the userâs genitalia, and comprising an opening providing access to the pouch, 18. The method of claim 17, wherein attaching the chamois to the body comprises partially attaching the chamois to the body. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein partially attaching the chamois to the body comprises: attaching at least a portion of the front panel to the body; and attaching at least a portion of the rear portion to the body.
